_comment: HF Channel Model Preset Configurations based on NTIA Report 90-255 (Vogler & Hoffmeyer, 1990)
_reference: Table 1 and Figures 4-9 in the paper provide example parameters for various ionospheric conditions

equatorial._description: Equatorial path - Single F-layer mode with large delay spread. Typical of trans-equatorial HF propagation. Reference: Paper Table 1, Figure 4

polar._description: Polar path - Multiple modes (E-layer and F-layer). Larger Doppler spreads due to auroral activity. Reference: Paper Table 1, Figure 5

midlatitude._description: Mid-latitude short path - Narrow delay spread, very small Doppler spread (stable ionosphere). Shows delay-Doppler coupling. Reference: Paper Table 1, Figure 6

auroral_spread_f._description: Auroral spread-F - Very large delay spread with spread-F enabled for diffuse scattering. Typical of disturbed ionospheric conditions. Reference: Paper Table 1, Figures 7-8

auroral_complex._description: Auroral complex - Multiple overlapping modes, narrow individual spreads but complex combined response. Reference: Paper Table 1, Figure 9

watterson_narrowband._description: Watterson-compatible narrowband mode - Approximates CCIR Report 549 Watterson model for narrowband signals (<12 kHz). Useful for comparison with legacy models.

benign._description: Benign conditions - Minimal distortion for testing. Very small delay and Doppler spreads.

severe._description: Severe conditions - Maximum distortion for stress testing. Very large delay and Doppler spreads.

parameter_descriptions.sample_rate: Sample rate in Hz. Higher rates needed for wider bandwidth signals.
parameter_descriptions.spread_f_enabled: Enable spread-F simulation (random amplitude modulation) for disturbed ionosphere.
parameter_descriptions.modes: Array of propagation modes (E-layer, F-layer low-ray, F-layer high-ray).

mode_parameters.name: Descriptive name for the mode
mode_parameters.amplitude: A - Relative mode amplitude [0, 1]. Paper Eq. (3).
mode_parameters.floor_amplitude: A_fl - Receiver threshold/floor level. Paper Eq. (4d), (5d).
mode_parameters.tau_L: Minimum delay time in microseconds. Paper Eq. (3).
mode_parameters.sigma_tau: Total delay spread = tau_U - tau_L in microseconds. Paper Eq. (3), Table 1.
mode_parameters.sigma_c: Carrier delay subinterval = tau_c - tau_L in microseconds. Paper Eq. (3).
mode_parameters.sigma_D: Doppler spread half-width in Hz. Paper Eq. (7), (10), Table 1.
mode_parameters.doppler_shift: f_s - Doppler shift at carrier delay in Hz. Paper Eq. (15).
mode_parameters.doppler_shift_min_delay: f_sL - Doppler shift at minimum delay in Hz. Paper Eq. (15b).

typical_values._comment: Typical parameter ranges from Table 1 in the paper
